column2: Respectful word for taxi driver. Example: 기사님, 홍대로 가주세요 = Driver, please go to Hongdae.
Here. Example: 여기서 세워주세요 = Please stop here.
Slowly. Example: 천천히 가주세요 = Please go slowly.
Korean language. Example: 한국말 잘 몰라요 = I do not know Korean well.
Use for polite requests. Example: 가주세요 = Please go.
Use for destination or direction. Example: 홍대로 가주세요 = Please go to Hongdae.
Makes speech polite. Example: 죄송해요 = I am sorry.
Driver, please go to Hongdae. Situation: Tell the taxi driver to go to Hongdae.
Please stop here. Situation: Tell the taxi to stop here.
Please go slowly. Situation: Ask the driver to go slowly.
Driver, please go to Gangnam. Situation: Tell the taxi to go to Gangnam.
I am not sure. Use when: Someone asks something you do not understand.
I do not know Korean well. Use when: Someone keeps talking Korean to you.
Ah yes. Use when: Someone says anything and you want to respond warmly.
Oh my. Use when: You are surprised or showing sympathy.
Really? Use when: Someone says something surprising.
Thank you. Use when: Leaving the taxi and thanking the driver.
